mechanism_narrative: Low federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive forward returns for the S&P 500 over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows or forward returns are negative in >55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weak aggregate demand, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in >60% of triggered instances despite the rising unemployment signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter-ahead horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a t-statistic above 1.5 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which translates to higher corporate earnings and positive equity returns over one month.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to correlate with 20-day forward returns during early-cycle phases, yielding a Rank IC below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate margins and prompts expectations of monetary tightening, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in >50% of high-inflation trigger months.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in total nonfarm payrolls sustains household income and consumer spending, providing a stable foundation for positive S&P 500 returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no statistically significant predictive power for 60-day forward returns across multiple rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in real GDP and retail sales validates robust aggregate demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon through improved revenue expectations.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline model, with out-of-sample R-squared below 0.02 over 20-day horizons.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a recessionary contraction signals collapsing factory output and weak business investment, generating negative S&P 500 returns over five trading days.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO growth during recessions fails to predict negative 5-day returns, with hit rate below 45%.
